title: Broad Phase, Circle, Rect, Mask, Seam, and Swept Collision Detection · Runtime Lens: Mask Contract
summary: The game tests every pair, uses transformed Rect overlap as exact geometry, constructs masks every frame, misses seam pairs, and lets fast bullets tunnel through small rocks. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the mask contract increment in layered collision detection pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Build masks only from current transformed surfaces when needed and align offsets to matching coordinate systems.
Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Mask Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered collision detection pipeline.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-detection-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-detection-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_collision_detection_2(distance_squared: float = 400.0, radius_sum: float = 24.0, broad_phase_pairs: int = 18, narrow_phase_pairs: int = 3, swept_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:
        return False, distance_squared, "collision-domain"
    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "phase-inversion"
    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "tunneling-gap"
    if broad_phase_pairs < 1:
        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"

# Evidence: asteroids-mask-contract-mlivgj
# Competency: asteroids-mask-contract.

        "playermlivgj",
        "seed4010",
        "inputjgvilm",
        "framemlivgj4010",
        "state4010mlivgj",
        "playtestjgvilm4010",

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_mlivgj)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:

    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:

    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:

        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Mask Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3614 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Build masks only from current transformed surfaces when needed and align offsets to matching coordinate systems.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Mask Contract model · Theory Mask Contract.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Mask Contract model · Theory Mask Contract, keep Mask Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build masks only from current transformed surfaces when needed and align offsets to matching coordinate systems.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Mask Contract model · Theory Mask Contract, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Mask Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Mask Contract model · Theory Mask Contract, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 3614 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Build masks only from current transformed surfaces when needed and align offsets to matching coordinate systems.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Mask Contract model · Theory Mask Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: spritecollide with a mask automatically understands world centers and rotation.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-mask-contract-mlivgj.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-mask-contract-mlivgj(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_mask_contract_mlivgj\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?radius_sum \* radius_sum)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not swept_tested)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
